scenario: A new agent joins a customer-support team. They open Tasklist. The team uses candidateGroups for User Tasks. The new agent should see all unassigned tasks they can work on.
question: What does Tasklist show by default?

options.text: Tasklist shows only tasks where the assignee field exactly matches the current user's identity. This is only partially true -- Tasklist does show tasks assigned to the user, but it also shows tasks for which the user is a candidate via candidateGroups or candidateUsers membership. A new agent whose group memberships include purchasing-managers would see all unassigned tasks for that group, not just tasks explicitly assigned to them.

Tasklist shows all tasks across all processes and all users in the entire cluster by default. Displaying all cluster tasks to every user would violate security and privacy requirements in multi-team or multi-tenant environments. Tasklist filters the task list based on user identity -- showing only tasks assigned to that user or tasks in groups the user belongs to. Cross-team task visibility is governed by candidateGroups and Identity configuration.

A new agent must manually configure their Tasklist view filters before any tasks appear in their queue. Tasklist applies identity-based filtering automatically based on the logged-in user's identity and group memberships without requiring manual filter configuration. Once the user is added to the appropriate group in Camunda Identity, their Tasklist view immediately shows candidate group tasks and any directly assigned tasks without additional setup.
" A new customer-support agent automatically sees all claimable tasks for their groups alongside any tasks directly assigned to them, enabling them to start working immediately without configuration.

a.text: Incorrect. Tasklist filters by user identity -- assigned tasks + candidate tasks for groups the user belongs to. New agent sees claimable work immediately.

c.text: Incorrect. Showing all cluster tasks would violate isolation.

d.text: Correct. Built-in filtering applies without setup.

findings.message: option text contains its own verdict/rationale
